output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what deductions are illegal, and how to demand your deposit back in 60 seconds.
content_outline: Introduction: The Real Cost of a Withheld Security Deposit
Is Your Landlord Allowed to Keep Your Deposit? Know Your Rights
Common Illegal Deductions: Cleaning, Normal Wear and Tear, and Vague Fees
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Give Your Deposit Back
How a Security Deposit Demand Letter Changes the Game
Escalating Your Claim: Small Claims Court and Legal Remedies
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a strict state-mandated window, typically 14 to 45 days.
Normal wear and tear, routine cleaning, and hypothetical damages are not legally deductible in most states.
A formal demand letter is often the fastest, most effective way to recover your deposit without hiring a lawyer.
Deposits-Back.com generates a professional security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee with no sign-up or subscription required.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your official demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no sign-up, no subscription, no stress.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who are problem-aware, have had their security deposit withheld or not returned by their landlord, and are actively searching for fast, affordable legal help online.
